title: Romantic Silky Cantonese Oysters Wrap
description: Succulent oysters wrapped in fragrant ti leaf, glazed with a silky soy-sugar sauce that melts in your mouth. A romantic dish that balances delicate flavor with a hint of spice.
instructions: Shuck the oysters and rinse them gently with cold water.
In a small saucepan, combine soy sauce, shaoxing wine, sesame oil, minced ginger, brown sugar, water, and chili flakes and simmer until the mixture thickens slightly.
Place a single oyster on each ti leaf square and pour a measured spoonful of the sauce over it.
Fold the ti leaf around the oyster, tucking the edges to form a compact wrap, and secure with a toothpick if needed.
Heat a nonstick pan over medium heat and place the wrapped oysters seam-side down, cooking for 3-4 minutes until the shell is lightly toasted.
Flip the wraps and cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing the sauce to caramelize and the oyster to finish cooking.
Remove from heat and arrange the wraps on a serving platter, drizzling any remaining sauce over the top.
Garnish with sliced scallions for a fresh contrast of color and flavor.
Serve immediately to enjoy the silky, yeasty romance of the dish.
